quick_facts.label: Conducting authority
quick_facts.value: KCET is the Karnataka Common Entrance Test (also described in KEA materials as UGCET/CET), conducted by the Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA).

quick_facts.label: Engineering purpose
quick_facts.value: For the 2026–27 cycle, KCET was used to determine eligibility and merit for first-year/first-semester Government-share seats in Engineering and Technology, among other professional courses, in Karnataka institutions.

quick_facts.label: Engineering subjects
quick_facts.value: Engineering/Technology applicants take Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics (PCM) in CET; the 2026 timetable scheduled Physics and Chemistry on April 23, 2026, and Mathematics on April 24, 2026.

quick_facts.label: Test structure
quick_facts.value: In the 2026 bulletin, each subject paper was an 80-minute, 60-mark objective multiple-choice paper with four answer options per question; incorrect answers did not attract negative marking.

quick_facts.label: Engineering rank basis
quick_facts.value: For Engineering/Technology, the 2026 merit/rank rule used Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics marks in KCET and in the qualifying examination in equal proportions.

sections.heading: What KCET is for
paragraphs: KCET is Karnataka’s state professional-course entrance and admissions process administered by KEA. In the 2026–27 bulletin, KEA described its role as determining eligibility and merit for Government-share seats in first-year or first-semester Engineering, Technology, Architecture, BNYS, Nursing, Veterinary, Farm Science, Pharmacy and related courses in Government, University, aided and unaided professional institutions in Karnataka.

For the engineering pathway, KCET is not simply an examination score: the rank is used in KEA’s online seat-allotment process, alongside the Government-issued seat matrix and the candidate’s ordered college/course options. A verification slip is required before a candidate can enter options.
bullets: For B.E./B.Tech, take the PCM CET papers.
Architecture uses NATA rather than the CET written paper for its entrance score, although candidates must register with KEA to participate in KEA counselling.
Medical, dental and AYUSH admissions use NEET; they are not admissions based on the KCET PCM paper.

sections.heading: Engineering eligibility
paragraphs: Under the 2026 KCET bulletin, an Engineering/Technology candidate needed to pass the qualifying examination (2nd PUC/12th standard or equivalent) with Physics and Mathematics as compulsory subjects, plus Chemistry, Biotechnology, Biology, Electronics or Computer Science as an optional subject, and English as a language of study. The stated minimum aggregate was 45% in the relevant optional subjects, reduced to 40% for the listed Karnataka SC, ST, Category-I and OBC categories.

Candidates awaiting their 2026 qualifying-examination result could apply and sit CET, but could not be admitted unless they subsequently passed the qualifying examination with the required percentage. Eligibility for Government seats is also subject to the CET-2006 Admission Rules, the applicable eligibility clause, document verification, and approvals or rules issued by competent bodies and the Government.
bullets: The 2026 bulletin states that there was no separate minimum CET mark prescribed for Engineering/Technology eligibility.
For Engineering rank preparation, KEA uses PCM marks; the flexibility to substitute an optional subject for Chemistry applies to eligibility, not rank calculation.

Category concessions and Government-seat eligibility are rule-bound and can depend on the candidate’s applicable Karnataka eligibility clause; read the current bulletin rather than relying on a prior year’s category rule.

sections.heading: Exam format for the engineering route
paragraphs: The 2026 KCET written test ran across two days and four sessions: Physics and Chemistry on the first day, then Mathematics and Biology on the second. Engineering/Technology candidates were required to appear in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics.

Each subject paper was 80 minutes and worth 60 marks. Papers were objective multiple-choice questions, each with four options and one mark. KEA stated that there was no negative evaluation for wrong answers, but no marks would be awarded if more than one answer was marked for a question.

KCET uses OMR answer sheets. KEA instructs candidates to use blue or black ballpoint pen, fill the identifying details and version code correctly, and shade one response per question; an incorrect CET number or version code can prevent scanning and rank assignment.
bullets: Engineering paper set: Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics.
2026 per-paper format: 60 marks, 80 minutes, MCQ.
Marking: one mark per question; no negative marking; multiple marked responses receive no mark.

sections.heading: Application, verification and counselling
paragraphs: The process is conducted through KEA’s online portal. In the 2026 cycle, KEA required online registration, application completion and payment, followed by verification of original documents according to the claim certificates. After verification, candidates holding a verification slip could log in, enter and prioritise course/college options, and participate in online seat allotment.

Deadlines are annual and variable. As dated historical context only, the 2026 bulletin opened registration on January 17, 2026, set February 16, 2026 as the online-application deadline and February 18, 2026 as the online-fee deadline; those dates have passed. Candidates for a subsequent cycle should use KEA’s current portal and current information bulletin rather than these dates.

Information entered in the online application is treated by KEA as the candidate’s responsibility. The bulletin also warns that applying, taking the exam or qualifying does not itself create an admission right: eligibility, supporting certificates and document verification remain necessary.
bullets: Register and pay through the KEA portal for the applicable cycle.
Keep qualifying-exam, study/reservation and other claim documents ready for verification, where applicable.
Obtain the verification slip before entering seat-allotment options.
Enter options in genuine preference order and follow the round-specific allotment instructions published by KEA.

sections.heading: Preparation priorities
paragraphs: Base academic preparation on the Karnataka first- and second-PUC syllabus. KEA states that the CET question papers are based on the First and Second PUC syllabi prescribed by Karnataka’s Department of Pre-University Education, with the CET syllabus published on the KEA website.

For B.E./B.Tech, prepare PCM together with 12th-standard performance: the engineering rank uses CET PCM and qualifying-examination PCM marks in equal proportions. This makes board/PUC preparation material to the final rank, not merely a separate eligibility step.

Practise the OMR workflow as well as questions. KEA specifically advises candidates to download and familiarise themselves with the specimen OMR sheet, including correct entry of the name, CET number and question-booklet version code.
bullets: Study the notified first- and second-PUC Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics syllabus.
Use timed 80-minute, 60-question practice sets for each subject.
Practise OMR marking carefully with a blue or black ballpoint pen and one selected bubble per question.
Check the current KEA noticeboard for the current-cycle syllabus, hall-ticket instructions and permitted-item rules.

sections.heading: Results, rank and seat allotment
paragraphs: For Engineering/Technology, KEA prepares rank using PCM marks from both KCET and the qualifying examination in equal proportions. If candidates are tied, the 2026 rule resolves engineering ties first using CET Mathematics marks, then CET Physics, then the relevant qualifying-examination marks, then age; a draw of lots applies if the specified criteria remain tied.

After the test, KEA publishes a provisional answer key and accepts online objections with supporting justification on the notified schedule. KEA then applies the expert-approved answer key for merit determination and publishes separate eligible-candidate rank/result sheets, including an Engineering rank list.

A rank supports participation in KEA allotment but is not a guarantee of a seat. Allotment depends on eligibility, verification, rank/merit, the published seat matrix, the candidate’s entered options and the applicable rules. KEA’s 2026 bulletin described online allotment in up to three rounds or as directed by apex bodies or Government.
bullets: Check the provisional key and submit any objection only by KEA’s notified method and time.
Check qualifying-exam marks displayed with the result; report discrepancies under KEA’s notified process.
Use the verification slip and ranked option list to participate in allotment.
Treat an allotment as provisional until all applicable institutional, university, regulatory and Government requirements are satisfied.
